title: Sampling two-dimensional spin systems with transformers
authors: Piotr Białas, Piotr Korcyl, Tomasz Stebel, Adam Stefański, Dawid Zapolski
abstract: Autoregressive Neural Networks based on dense or convolutional layers have recently been shown to be a viable strategy for generating classical spin systems. Unlike these methods, sampling with transformers is commonly considered to be computationally inefficient. In this work, we propose a novel approach to transformer-based neural samplers in which we generate not a single spin per step but groups of spins. As an additional improvement, we construct a model of approximated probabilities, further improving the efficiency of the algorithm. Despite our approach being computationally heavier than dense networks or CNN-based approaches, we were able to sample larger systems of up to $180 \times 180$ spins in case of the Ising model. The Effective Sample Size of our sampler is $\sim 20$ times larger than that of the previous state-of-the-art neural sampler when trained for the $128 \times 128$ Ising model at critical temperature. Finally, we also test our algorithm on the 2D Edwards-Anderson model, where we train $64\times 64$ spin systems.
summary: The authors present an efficient transformer-based neural sampler for 2D classical spin systems by generating groups of spins rather than single spins. This method significantly outperforms previous neural samplers in terms of Effective Sample Size, even when applied to larger system sizes and disordered models like the Edwards-Anderson model.
main_problem: The computational inefficiency of using transformer architectures for sampling classical spin systems compared to dense or convolutional neural networks.
main_result: A novel transformer-based sampler that generates groups of spins simultaneously and uses approximated probabilities, achieving an Effective Sample Size 20 times larger than previous state-of-the-art samplers for the 128x128 Ising model at critical temperature.
method: An autoregressive transformer-based approach that employs group-based spin generation and a model of approximated probabilities to improve sampling efficiency.
model_or_system: The study focuses on two-dimensional classical spin systems, specifically the Ising model and the 2D Edwards-Anderson spin glass model.
important_parameters: System sizes up to 180x180 for the Ising model and 64x64 for the Edwards-Anderson model; critical temperature for the Ising model.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a new transformer-based sampling architecture, describes the group-generation and probability approximation techniques, and evaluates performance on the Ising and Edwards-Anderson models.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
